record.premise: A rhyming picture book that counts and describes colorful fish, then introduces a parade of silly invented creatures with rhyming names doing silly things, page by page.

characters.name: Nook
characters.role: Unusual animal/creature

characters.name: Wump
characters.role: Creature with seven humps that you can ride

characters.name: Yink
characters.role: Creature who likes to wink and drink pink ink

characters.name: Yap
characters.role: Unusual animal/creature

characters.name: Gack
characters.role: Unusual animal/creature

characters.name: Zeds
characters.role: Unusual animal/creature

characters.name: Mike
characters.role: Creature with many legs who pedals his bike while others sit on top

characters.name: Yop
characters.role: Creature on top

characters.name: Zans
characters.role: Creatures that brush their teeth

characters.name: Yeps
characters.role: Creature

characters.name: Clark
characters.role: Character who never goes to school

characters.name: Mr. Gump
characters.role: A Wump with seven humps

characters.name: Zeep
characters.role: Creature the children put their cold feet on at bedtime to keep warm

characters.name: Narrator
characters.role: Narrator who counts and describes fish and creatures

key_objects.detail: Red, blue, black, old, new; some sad, some glad, some bad
Some fish have a little star
Some fish have a little car

plot_beats.beat: The narrator counts fish: one fish, two fish
The narrator describes colorful fish: red fish, blue fish, black fish, old fish, new fish
Fish are described by their feelings: some are sad, some are glad, some are bad
Fish are described by what they have: some have a little star, some have a little car
A parade of silly invented creatures with rhyming names is introduced
The Wump with seven humps is shown as something you can ride
Mike with many legs pedals his bike while others sit on top of him
The Yink winks and drinks pink ink
The Zans brush their teeth
At bedtime, the narrator and children put their cold feet on the Zeep to keep warm

record.setting: Imaginative/fantasy world with colorful fish and silly invented creatures

specific_facts.fact: The Wump has seven humps
The Yink likes to wink and drink pink ink
Mike has many legs and pedals his bike
The Zans brush their teeth
Clark never goes to school
Mr. Gump is a Wump with seven humps
Children put their cold feet on the Zeep at bedtime to keep warm
Some fish are sad, some are glad, some are bad
Some fish have a little star and some have a little car

record.premise: Eight-year-old Jack and his seven-year-old sister Annie discover a magic tree house in the woods that transports them back in time to the age of dinosaurs, where they have adventures and find a mysterious gold medallion.

characters.name: Jack
characters.role: Main character, eight-year-old boy, Annie's older brother

characters.name: Annie
characters.role: Main character, seven-year-old girl, Jack's younger sister

characters.name: Pteranodon
characters.role: Friendly flying dinosaur who helps Jack escape from the T-Rex

characters.name: Triceratops
characters.role: Peaceful dinosaur the children encounter

characters.name: Anatosaurus
characters.role: Duck-billed dinosaur the children encounter

characters.name: T-Rex
characters.role: Fearsome dinosaur who chases Jack

key_objects.detail: Located high in an oak tree, full of books
Used to climb up to the tree house
Jack picks it up and wishes to see a dinosaur
Has the letter M engraved on it

plot_beats.beat: Jack and Annie are walking in the woods near their home when Annie spots a tree house high in an oak tree
They climb up to the tree house using a rope ladder and find it full of books
Jack picks up a book about dinosaurs and wishes he could see one
The tree house starts to spin and transports them to a prehistoric world
They meet friendly dinosaurs including a Pteranodon, Triceratops, and Anatosaurus
A fearsome T-Rex chases Jack
The Pteranodon helps Jack escape by carrying him through the air back to the tree house
Jack and Annie find a gold medallion with the letter M engraved on it
They wish to go home and the tree house spins again, returning them to Frog Creek
Jack keeps the medallion as proof of their adventure

record.setting: The town of Frog Creek, Pennsylvania, and a prehistoric world with dinosaurs

specific_facts.fact: Jack is eight years old
Annie is seven years old
Annie is Jack's younger sister
Jack and Annie live in Frog Creek, Pennsylvania
The tree house is in an oak tree
The gold medallion has the letter M engraved on it
The Pteranodon is described as a friendly herbivore
The Triceratops is described as peaceful
The Anatosaurus is described as duck-billed
Hardly any time has passed at home when they return
Jack keeps the medallion as proof of their adventure

unknown_fields: identity of the mysterious owner of the tree house (only hinted with letter M)
what grade Jack and Annie are in at school
how long they spend in the dinosaur world
what other books are in the tree house
what Jack and Annie look like (hair color, clothing)
time of day when they find the tree house
season when the story takes place

record.premise: A goose escapes from its pen on a farm and a boy must chase it and find a clever way to get it back.

characters.name: Goose
characters.role: Main character who escapes from its pen

characters.name: Boy
characters.role: Chases the goose and tries to get it back to its pen

characters.name: Pig
characters.role: Farm animal the goose runs past

characters.name: Cow
characters.role: Farm animal the goose runs past

characters.name: Sheep
characters.role: Farm animal the goose runs past

characters.name: Duck
characters.role: Farm animal the goose runs past

key_objects.detail: Used by the boy to chase the goose
Where the goose escapes from and returns to
Food used to lead the goose back to its pen

plot_beats.beat: A goose escapes from its pen on a farm
A boy chases the goose with a broom
The goose runs past the pig, the cow, the sheep, and the duck
The animals all watch the chase
The goose is too fast for the boy to catch
The boy gets clever and uses corn to lead the goose
The goose calmly follows the corn back to its pen
The boy is happy and the goose is happy too

specific_facts.fact: The goose escapes from a pen
The boy uses a broom to chase the goose
The goose runs past four animals: pig, cow, sheep, and duck
The boy uses corn (food) to lead the goose back
The goose returns to its pen at the end

unknown_fields: exact words or rhymes used in the book
